author: BI Chamber
title: Autumn Andy’s Way Walk
summary: James Stover Exploration Series
content:
James Stover Exploration Series

Come for its beauty (not seining or bird scopeing). Be prepared to get your feet a little wet. Meet at Andy’s Way parking area.

Advance sign-up recommended but not required, email Kim at kim.gaffett@tnc.org.

The James Stover Block Island Exploration Series

Discover the fascinating nature of Block Island. Guided monthly walks for families (with kids of all ages) and individuals. Explore areas and species special to Block Island.  These walks are funded by friends of James Stover (1996-2003), in memory of a boy who loved exploring Block Island.

For more info and the complete schedule of nature walks and programs, please visit  www.natureblockisland.org
address: Andy's Way New Shoreham Washington County

author: Austin Crawford
title: Block Island Race
summary: Looking for a way to warm up your season and practice the Around the Island Race? There is always the Block Island Race in May!
content:
Looking for a way to warm up your season and practice the Around the Island Race? There is always the Block Island Race in May!

The Block Island Race was first held in 1946 and is a qualifier for the North Ocean Racing Trophy (ORC), the Double Handed Ocean Racing Trophy (ORC), the New England Lighthouse Series (PHRF) and the Gulf Stream Series (ORC). The Block Island Race is also a qualifier for the Caper, Sagola and Windigo trophies awarded by the YRA of Long Island Sound and the “Tuna Trophy” for the best combined ORC scores in the Edlu and Storm Trysail Block Island Race.
